properties.description: Timestamp of when the action occurred.

properties.name: softwareAgent
properties.description: The software agent that performed the action.

properties.name: changed
properties.description: A semicolon-delimited list of the parts of the resource that were changed since the previous event history. If not present, presumed to be undefined. When tracking changes and the scope of the changed components is unknown, it should be assumed that anything might have changed.

properties.name: instanceID
properties.description: The value of the xmpMM:InstanceID property for the modified (output) resource

properties.name: parameters
properties.description: Additional parameters of the action. These will often vary by the type of action

properties.name: actors
properties.description: An array of the creators that undertook this action

properties.name: digitalSourceType
properties.description: One of the defined source types at https://cv.iptc.org/newscodes/digitalsourcetype/

properties.description: Ratings given to the assertion (may be empty)

properties.name: dateTime
properties.description: The ISO 8601 date-time string when the assertion was created/generated

properties.name: reference
properties.description: hashed_uri reference to another assertion that this review is about

properties.name: dataSource
properties.description: A description of the source of the assertion data, selected from a predefined list

properties.name: localizations
properties.description: localizations for strings in the assertion

relationships.description: A description of the source of the assertion data, selected from a predefined list

properties.name: details
properties.description: A human readable string giving details about the source of the assertion data, e.g. the URL of the remote server that provided the data

properties.name: actors
properties.description: array of hashed_uri references to W3C Verifiable Credentials

properties.name: code
properties.description: A label-formatted string that describes the reason for the rating

properties.name: explanation
properties.description: A human readable string explaining why the rating is what it is

relationships.name: code
relationships.description: A label-formatted string that describes the reason for the rating

properties.name: exclusions
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute this hash, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken from an enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hashes in this Merkle tree, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken the `alg` value of the enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: hash
properties.description: For non-fragmented MP4, this shall be the hash of the entire BMFF file excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For `c2pa.hash.bmff`  assertions, if the merkle field is present, this hash also excludes all 'mdat' boxes.  For fragmented MP4, this field is required to be absent.

properties.name: merkle
properties.description: A set of Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable verification of a single 'mdat' box, multiple 'mdat' boxes, and/or individual fragment files within the asset.

properties.name: name
properties.description: optional) a human-readable description of what this hash covers.

properties.name: url
properties.description: (optional) a file or http(s) URL to where the bytes that are being hashed lived.  This is useful for cases where the data lives in a different file chunk or side-car than the claim.

properties.name: xpath
properties.description: Location of box(es) to exclude from the hash starting from the root node as an xpath formatted string of version https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-10/ with highly constrained syntax.

properties.name: length
properties.description: (optional) Length that a leafmost box must have to exclude from the hash.

properties.name: data
properties.description: (optional) The data in the leafmost box at the specified relative byte offset must be identical to the specified data for the box to be excluded from the hash.

properties.name: subset
properties.description: (optional) Only this portion of the excluded box shall be excluded from the hash.  Each entry in the array must have a monotonically increasing relative byte offset.  No subset within the array may overlap.  The last entry may have a length of zero; this indicates that the remainder of the box from that relative byte offset onward is excluded.  A relative byte offset or relative byte offset plus length that exceeds the length of the box is allowed; bytes beyond the end of the box are never hashed.

properties.name: version
properties.description: (optional) Version that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Shall only be specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

properties.name: flags
properties.description: (optional) byte string of exactly 3 bytes.  The 24-bit flags that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Shall only be specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

properties.name: exact
properties.description: (optional) indicates that flags must be an exact match.  If not specified, defaults to true.  Shall only be specified for a box that inherits from FullBox and when flags is also specified.

relationships.name: flags
relationships.description: (optional) byte string of exactly 3 bytes.  The 24-bit flags that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Shall only be specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Each entry in a map is a Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable validation of a single

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Each entry in a map is a Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable validation of a single

properties.name: length
properties.description: (optional) Length that a leafmost box must have to exclude from the hash.

MerkleMap.name: MerkleMap

properties.name: localId
properties.description: Local id used to differentiate across multiple 'mdat' boxes within a single file to determine which Merkle tree should be used to validate that 'mdat' box.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Number of leaf nodes in the Merkle tree.  Null nodes are not included in this count.

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hashes in this Merkle tree, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken the `alg` value of the enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: initHash
properties.description: For fragmented MP4 assets which are split across multiple files, this field is required to be present and shall be the hash of the entire initialization segment file for chunks hashed by this Merkle tree excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For fragmented MP4 assets which are stored as a single flat MP4 file, this field is required to be present and shall be the hash of all bytes preceding the first 'moof' box excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For non-fragmented MP4, this field is required to be absent.

properties.name: hashes
properties.description: An ordered array representing a single row of the Merkle tree which may be the leaf-most row, root row, or any intermediate row.  The depth of the row is implied by (shall be computed from) the number of items in this array.

MerkleMap.description: Each entry in a map is a Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable validation of a single 'mdat' box or multiple 'mdat' boxes within the asset.",

BmffMerkleMap.name: BmffMerkleMap

properties.name: localId
properties.description: A local id indicating which 'mdat' box this entry pertains to.  This may not be globally unique

properties.name: location
properties.description: Zero-based index into the leaf-most Merkle tree row corresponding to this 'mdat' box or portion of this 'mdat' box

properties.name: hashes
properties.description: An ordered array representing the set of additional hashes required to reach a hash in the Merkle tree specified in the manifest from leaf-most (peer of this node) to root-most (child of node in manifest).  Note that this array may not be present, e.g. if the manifest itself contains the leaf-most row of the Merkle tree.  Null hashes are not included in this array.  The algorithm used shall be determined using the `alg` field from the corresponding entry in the `merkle` field array in the BMFF hash structure.

BmffMerkleMap.description: The data structure used to store sufficient information to validate a single 'mdat' box or a portion of an 'mdat' box when a Merkle tree is used",

properties.name: redacted_assertions

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute all data hash assertions listed in this claim unless otherwise overridden, taken from the C2PA data hash algorithm identifier registry. This provides the value for the 'alg' field in data-hash and hashed-uri structures contained in this claim

properties.name: alg_soft
properties.description: A string identifying the algorithm used to compute all soft binding assertions listed in this claim unless otherwise overridden, taken from the C2PA soft binding algorithm identifier registry."

properties.description: additional information about the assertion

relationships.description: additional information about the assertion

DepthmapGdepthMap.name: DepthmapGdepthMap

properties.name: GDepth:Format
properties.description: The format that describes how to convert the depthmap data into a valid float-point depthmap. Current valid values are 'RangeInverse' and 'RangeLinear'

properties.name: GDepth:Near
properties.description: The near value of the depthmap in depth units

properties.name: GDepth:Far
properties.description: The far value of the depthmap in depth units

properties.name: GDepth:Mime
properties.description: The mime type for the base64 string describing the depth image content, e.g. 'image/jpeg' or 'image/png'",

properties.name: GDepth:Data
properties.description: The base64 encoded depth image. Please see GDepth encoding page at developers.google.com. The depthmap will be stretched-to-fit the corresponding color image

properties.name: GDepth:Units
properties.description: The units of the depthmap, e.g. 'm' for meters or 'mm' for millimeters

properties.name: GDepth:MeasureType
properties.description: The type of depth measurement. Current valid values are 'OpticalAxis' and 'OpticRay

properties.name: GDepth:ConfidenceMime
properties.description: The mime type for the base64 string describing the confidence image content, e.g. 'image/png'.",

properties.name: GDepth:Confidence
properties.description: The base64 encoded confidence image. Please see GDepth encoding page at developers.google.com. The confidence map should have the same size as the depthmap

properties.name: GDepth:Manufacturer
properties.description: The manufacturer of the device that created this depthmap

properties.name: GDepth:Model
properties.description: The model of the device that created this depthmap

properties.name: GDepth:Software
properties.description: The software that created this depthmap

properties.name: GDepth:ImageWidth
properties.description: The width in pixels of the original color image associated to this depthmap. This is NOT the depthmap width. If present, apps must update this property when scaling, cropping or rotating the color image. Clients use this property to verify the integrity of the depthmap w.r.t. the color image

properties.name: GDepth:ImageHeight
properties.description: The height in pixels of the original color image associated to this depthmap. This is NOT the depthmap height. If present, apps must update this property when scaling, cropping or rotating the color image. Clients use this property to verify the integrity of the depthmap w.r.t. the color image

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: additional information about the assertion

SchemaOrgThingSchema.name: SchemaOrgThingSchema

properties.name: additionalType
properties.description: An additional type for the item, typically used for adding more specific types from external vocabularies in microdata syntax. This is a relationship between something and a class that the thing is in. In RDFa syntax, it is better to use the native RDFa syntax - the 'typeof' attribute - for multiple types. Schema.org tools may have only weaker understanding of extra types, in particular those defined externally."

properties.name: alternateName
properties.description: An alias for the item

properties.name: description
properties.description: A Description for the item

properties.name: disambiguatingDescription
properties.description: "image": $image-type-choice, ; An image of the item. This can be a URL or a fully described ImageObject.

properties.name: mainEntityOfPage
properties.description: Indicates a page (or other CreativeWork) for which this thing is the main entity being described. See background notes for details. Inverse property mainEntity.

properties.name: name
properties.description: the name of the action

properties.name: potentialAction
properties.description: Indicates a potential Action, which describes an idealized action in which this thing would play an 'object' role

properties.name: sameAs
properties.description: URL of a reference Web page that unambiguously indicates the item's identity. E.g. the URL of the item's Wikipedia page, Freebase page, or official website."

properties.name: url
properties.description: url of the item

validation_rules.description: This validation must be completed before a validator presents a successful result to a human user or begins to render any content.

validation_rules.description: The code shall conform to the same syntax as custom labels, e.g. com.litware.
validation_rules.source_text: The code shall conform to the same syntax as custom labels, e.g. com.litware.

validation_rules.description: When using custom labels, because they are not inherently success or failure codes, a boolean success or failure result must be returned as well.
validation_rules.source_text: When using custom labels, because they are not inherently success or failure codes, a boolean success or failure result must be returned as well.

validation_rules.description: The last C2PA Manifest superbox in the C2PA Manifest Store superbox is the active manifest, but locating the C2PA Manifest Store may involve looking in a number possible locations.
validation_rules.source_text: The last C2PA Manifest superbox in the C2PA Manifest Store superbox is the active manifest, but locating the C2PA Manifest Store may involve looking in a number possible locations.

validation_rules.description: The manifest consumer shall look inside the asset for an embedded C2PA Manifest Store in the standard locations for embedding manifests to see if one is present.
validation_rules.source_text: The manifest consumer shall look inside the asset for an embedded C2PA Manifest Store in the standard locations for embedding manifests to see if one is present.

validation_rules.description: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores present in an asset, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.
validation_rules.source_text: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores present in an asset, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.

validation_rules.description:
In the case where this asset is being added as an ingredient, none of these embedded C2PA manifests shall be included in the ingredient assertion.
 
#### By Reference or URI
validation_rules.source_text:
In the case where this asset is being added as an ingredient, none of these embedded C2PA manifests shall be included in the ingredient assertion.
 
#### By Reference or URI

validation_rules.description: If there is no embedded C2PA Manifest Store, the following attempts should be made to locate one at a remote location.
validation_rules.source_text: If there is no embedded C2PA Manifest Store, the following attempts should be made to locate one at a remote location.

validation_rules.description: - If the asset was retrieved via an HTTP connection, the manifest consumer should look in the header of the HTTP response for a Link header, as defined in https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8288[RFC 8288], containing a parameter of rel=c2pa-manifest.
validation_rules.source_text: - If the asset was retrieved via an HTTP connection, the manifest consumer should look in the header of the HTTP response for a Link header, as defined in https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8288

validation_rules.description: - If the asset has XMP and the XMP contains a dcterms:provenance key, the provided URI should be used to locate the active manifest.
validation_rules.source_text: - If the asset has XMP and the XMP contains a dcterms:provenance key, the provided URI should be used to locate the active manifest.

validation_rules.description: - If no C2PA Manifest Store has been located, the manifest consumer should look for files at the same path or URI, but with a filename extension of .c2pa.
validation_rules.source_text: - If no C2PA Manifest Store has been located, the manifest consumer should look for files at the same path or URI, but with a filename extension of .c2pa.

validation_rules.description: If the C2PA Manifest Store is not found, a manifest consumer may look in whatever additional places it deems most appropriate to locate it.
validation_rules.source_text: If the C2PA Manifest Store is not found, a manifest consumer may look in whatever additional places it deems most appropriate to locate it.

validation_rules.description: A manifest consumer may wish to validate that the located C2PA Manifest Store is indeed the one associated with asset.
validation_rules.source_text: A manifest consumer may wish to validate that the located C2PA Manifest Store is indeed the one associated with asset.

validation_rules.description: If the C2PA Manifest Store was located then the hard binding assertion present in its active manifest shall be used to validate that it is the matching manifest and whether the asset has been modified without manifest updates.
validation_rules.source_text: If the C2PA Manifest Store was located then the hard binding assertion present in its active manifest shall be used to validate that it is the matching manifest and whether the asset has been modified

validation_rules.description: Accordingly, the manifest consumer shall treat this as a non-matching hard binding and reject the manifest with a failure code of assertion.dataHash.mismatch if a data hash assertion is used or assertion.bmffHash.mismatch if a BMFF hash assertion is used.
validation_rules.source_text: Accordingly, the manifest consumer shall treat this as a non-matching hard binding and reject the manifest with a failure code of assertion.dataHash.mismatch if a data hash assertion is used or assert

validation_rules.description: There shall only be one such box in the active manifest, if more than one is located, the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of claim.multiple.
validation_rules.source_text: There shall only be one such box in the active manifest, if more than one is located, the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of claim.multiple.

validation_rules.description: The signature must be embedded in the same manifest as described in .
validation_rules.source_text: The signature must be embedded in the same manifest as described in .

validation_rules.description: If the signature URI does not refer to a location within the same C2PA Manifest box (a self#jumbf location), the claim must be rejected.
validation_rules.source_text: If the signature URI does not refer to a location within the same C2PA Manifest box (a self#jumbf location), the claim must be rejected.

validation_rules.description: If no such field is present or the URI cannot be resolved, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of claimSignature.missing.
validation_rules.source_text: If no such field is present or the URI cannot be resolved, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of claimSignature.missing.

validation_rules.description: If a chain of trust cannot be built from the credential to an entry in the trust anchor list, the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.untrusted.
validation_rules.source_text: If a chain of trust cannot be built from the credential to an entry in the trust anchor list, the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.untrusted.

validation_rules.description: If the credential is not acceptable per the requirements of the credential's type, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.invalid.
validation_rules.source_text: If the credential is not acceptable per the requirements of the credential's type, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.invalid.

validation_rules.description: If the signature algorithm is not on the allowed or deprecated list in , then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of algorithm.unsupported.
validation_rules.source_text: If the signature algorithm is not on the allowed or deprecated list in , then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of algorithm.unsupported.

validation_rules.description: After confirming the credential type and signing algorithm are acceptable, validation should proceed according to the specified procedure in .
validation_rules.source_text: After confirming the credential type and signing algorithm are acceptable, validation should proceed according to the specified procedure in .

validation_rules.description: If validation of the signature fails, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of claimSignature.mismatch.
validation_rules.source_text: If validation of the signature fails, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of claimSignature.mismatch.

validation_rules.description: Unless otherwise specified in  or , a header may appear in either bucket. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8152#section-3[RFC8152 section 3] describes COSE headers.
validation_rules.source_text: Unless otherwise specified in  or , a header may appear in either bucket. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8152#section-3[RFC8152 section 3] describes COSE headers.

validation_rules.description: If it is not, the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.expired.
validation_rules.source_text: If it is not, the claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.expired.

validation_rules.description:
The claim must be rejected with a failure code of timeStamp.mismatch if it does not match.
* The time attested by the Time Stamp Authority (TSA) falls within the validity period of the signing credential.

* The time attested by the Time Stamp Authority (TSA) falls within the validity period of the signing credent

validation_rules.description:
The claim must be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.expired if it does not.
* The attested time falls within the validity period of the TSA's signing certificate, The claim must be rejected with a failure code of timeStamp.outsideValidity if it does not.
* A trust chain can be built to an entry in the TSA trust store as described in .

validation_rules.source_text: The claim must be rejected with a failure code of timeStamp.untrusted if a trust chain cannot be built.

validation_rules.description: * If the rVals header is present, its contents shall be validated as described in  for the signer credential type.
validation_rules.source_text: * If the rVals header is present, its contents shall be validated as described in  for the signer credential type.

validation_rules.description:
* If the rVals header is not present, and the signer's credential is considered valid by the requirements of  and , a validator may choose to query the credential status method to determine if the credential is currently revoked.
** If the validator does not query the credential status, the validator shall presume the credential is not revoked.
** If the validator does query the credential status, it shall determine the status from the response as described in  for the signer's credential type.

validation_rules.description: In all cases, if the credential is deemed revoked at the time of signing, the claim shall be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.revoked.
validation_rules.source_text: In all cases, if the credential is deemed revoked at the time of signing, the claim shall be rejected with a failure code of signingCredential.revoked.

validation_rules.description: A validator shall check for required and not-permitted assertions.
validation_rules.spec_section: Validate the correct assertions for the type of manifest
validation_rules.source_text: A validator shall check for required and not-permitted assertions.

validation_rules.description: For each manifest, there may be a set of its assertions that were redacted from it.
validation_rules.spec_section: Preparing the list of redacted assertions
validation_rules.source_text: For each manifest, there may be a set of its assertions that were redacted from it.

validation_rules.description: Therefore a validator, when processing a claim, shall gather the set of redacted assertion for each ingredient manifest based on each hashed_uri listed in the redacted_assertions field.
validation_rules.spec_section: Preparing the list of redacted assertions
validation_rules.source_text: Therefore a validator, when processing a claim, shall gather the set of redacted assertion for each ingredient manifest based on each hashed_uri listed in the redacted_assertions field.

validation_rules.description: A claim's redacted_assertions field shall never include a hashed_uri to any of its own assertions.
validation_rules.spec_section: Preparing the list of redacted assertions
validation_rules.source_text: A claim's redacted_assertions field shall never include a hashed_uri to any of its own assertions.

validation_rules.description: A validator must first successfully validate a claim before attempting to retrieve external data listed in a cloud data assertion.
validation_rules.source_text: A validator must first successfully validate a claim before attempting to retrieve external data listed in a cloud data assertion.

validation_rules.description: A validator must not attempt to retrieve external data from a rejected claim.
validation_rules.source_text: A validator must not attempt to retrieve external data from a rejected claim.

validation_rules.description: As the retrieval of external data is optional, the inability to retrieve or validate external data shall not cause a claim to become rejected.
validation_rules.source_text: As the retrieval of external data is optional, the inability to retrieve or validate external data shall not cause a claim to become rejected.

validation_rules.description: If a validator chooses to retrieve any of the external data in a cloud data assertion, the validator must:
validation_rules.source_text: If a validator chooses to retrieve any of the external data in a cloud data assertion, the validator must:

validation_rules.description: A validator must perform the above validation steps for the asset being presented and its manifest.
validation_rules.source_text: A validator must perform the above validation steps for the asset being presented and its manifest.

validation_rules.description: If any of the above steps conclude the manifest is invalid, that manifest must be rejected with the indicated failure code.
validation_rules.source_text: If any of the above steps conclude the manifest is invalid, that manifest must be rejected with the indicated failure code.

validation_rules.description: An asset's manifest may list one or more ingredients.
validation_rules.source_text: An asset's manifest may list one or more ingredients.

validation_rules.description: For standard manifests, a validator may choose to optionally recurse through any ingredient manifests, however the parentOf ingredient of an update manifest shall be validated by the procedure below.
validation_rules.source_text: For standard manifests, a validator may choose to optionally recurse through any ingredient manifests, however the parentOf ingredient of an update manifest shall be validated by the procedure below.

validation_rules.description: There is no requirement that signers of ingredient manifests are trusted by the validator (except in the case of endorsement validation), and building of trust chains of signers of ingredients shall not be attempted.

validation_rules.description: Applications should not display data from ingredient manifests with failed integrity checks.
validation_rules.source_text: Applications should not display data from ingredient manifests with failed integrity checks.

validation_rules.description: If the application chooses to display such data, it must flag the display with a warning about the failed integrity check, and that the data cannot be reliably attributed to the ingredient manifest's signer nor to the asset's manifest's signer.
validation_rules.source_text: If the application chooses to display such data, it must flag the display with a warning about the failed integrity check, and that the data cannot be reliably attributed to the ingredient manifest's

validation_rules.description: When ingredients are being added to an asset as part of an authoring workflow, the ingredient may undergo full validation and the results expressed in a validation status.
validation_rules.source_text: When ingredients are being added to an asset as part of an authoring workflow, the ingredient may undergo full validation and the results expressed in a validation status.

validation_rules.description: For consumption scenarios, it is expected that problems with ingredient manifests from a standard manifest would be ignored during normal consumption use but may be surfaced as a warning if a user opts to explore the provenance history.
validation_rules.source_text: For consumption scenarios, it is expected that problems with ingredient manifests from a standard manifest would be ignored during normal consumption use but may be surfaced as a warning if a user opt

validation_rules.description: However, the parentOf ingredient of an update manifest shall be surfaced.
validation_rules.source_text: However, the parentOf ingredient of an update manifest shall be surfaced.

validation_rules.description: In authoring scenarios, it may be desirable to more prominently raise warnings so that a creator making use of such an asset with a flawed provenance history can make an informed decision of how to proceed.

If the manifest is an update manifest, then the claim must be rejected with a failure code of manifest.update.wrongParents.
* If the ingredient does have a c2pa_manifest field:

If an alg field is not present in the hashed_uri structure, an alg field must be present in an enclosing structure, and the nearest instance present determines the hash algorithm.
..

validation_rules.description: If the ingredient contains a validationStatus field, each of the entries in the array shall be evaluated.
validation_rules.source_text: If the ingredient contains a validationStatus field, each of the entries in the array shall be evaluated.

validation_rules.description: An admitted manifest is treated like an accepted manifest, but with an explicit indication that it may contain validation errors that are known to the signer.
validation_rules.source_text: An admitted manifest is treated like an accepted manifest, but with an explicit indication that it may contain validation errors that are known to the signer.

validation_rules.description: If admitted in this way, the validator must present each validationStatus present in the validationStatus field array as part of any exploration of the provenance history.
validation_rules.source_text: If admitted in this way, the validator must present each validationStatus present in the validationStatus field array as part of any exploration of the provenance history.

validation_rules.description:
Validators should perform full validation if exploration of the provenance history of the ingredient is requested to indicate where there are validation errors.
+
[NOTE]
The presence of a validationStatus with a failure code or with a success field with a value of false is an explicit statement by the signer that they acknowledged and have chosen to override validation errors in the ingredient's claim itself.
+
.

The presence of a validationSt

validation_rules.description: The validator may optionally recursively validate the ingredient's ingredients.
validation_rules.source_text: The validator may optionally recursively validate the ingredient's ingredients.

validation_rules.description: Endorsement validation shall only be attempted if the active manifest first meets a number of conditions:
validation_rules.source_text: Endorsement validation shall only be attempted if the active manifest first meets a number of conditions:

validation_rules.description: In order to provide resilience in the case of a claim generator unexpectedly adding multiple endorsement assertions to an assertion store, a validator may iterate through the validation process below with each endorsement, stopping the iteration immediately if an endorsement validates successfully.
validation_rules.source_text: In order to provide resilience in the case of a claim generator unexpectedly adding multiple endorsement assertions to an assertion store, a validator may iterate through the validation process below

validation_rules.description: When endorsement validation has completed, for each actions assertion in the active manifest where all actions are marked as __endorsed__, the actions assertion itself shall be marked as __endorsed__ in the validation results returned by the validator.
validation_rules.source_text: When endorsement validation has completed, for each actions assertion in the active manifest where all actions are marked as __endorsed__, the actions assertion itself shall be marked as __endorsed__

validation_rules.description: If that manifest is also an update manifest, the search for a standard manifest shall recurse though the chain of ingredients.
validation_rules.source_text: If that manifest is also an update manifest, the search for a standard manifest shall recurse though the chain of ingredients.

validation_rules.description: If no standard manifest is found, then the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of manifest.update.wrongParents.
validation_rules.source_text: If no standard manifest is found, then the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of manifest.update.wrongParents.

validation_rules.description: Once a standard manifest (and its bindings) has been located, the exclusion range(s) shall be extracted from the c2pa.hash.data assertion.
validation_rules.source_text: Once a standard manifest (and its bindings) has been located, the exclusion range(s) shall be extracted from the c2pa.hash.data assertion.

validation_rules.description: If any update manifests were encountered then the length value of the exclusion range whose start value is the offset of the start of the entire C2PA manifest store shall be treated as the current length of the entire C2PA manifest store plus any file format specific extras.
validation_rules.source_text: If any update manifests were encountered then the length value of the exclusion range whose start value is the offset of the start of the entire C2PA manifest store shall be treated as the current len

validation_rules.description: The hash algorithm (alg) specified in that c2pa.hash.data shall be computed over the bytes of the asset, minus those specified in the exclusion range(s).
validation_rules.source_text: The hash algorithm (alg) specified in that c2pa.hash.data shall be computed over the bytes of the asset, minus those specified in the exclusion range(s).

validation_rules.description: If the resultant hash does not match the value of the hash field in the c2pa.hash.data, then the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of assertion.dataHash.mismatch.
validation_rules.source_text: If the resultant hash does not match the value of the hash field in the c2pa.hash.data, then the manifest shall be rejected with a failure code of assertion.dataHash.mismatch.

validation_rules.description: Because the segment lengths are inside the exclusion range, a validator shall match the total length of the exclusion range with that of the total length of all APP11 segments representing the C2PA Manifest to ensure that the length was not tampered with.
validation_rules.source_text: Because the segment lengths are inside the exclusion range, a validator shall match the total length of the exclusion range with that of the total length of all APP11 segments representing the C2PA Ma

validation_rules.description: For any portions of an asset rendered for presentation to a user, including but not limited to audio, video, or text, the corresponding hard binding corresponding to the rendered content must be validated in accordance with .
validation_rules.source_text: For any portions of an asset rendered for presentation to a user, including but not limited to audio, video, or text, the corresponding hard binding corresponding to the rendered content must be valid

validation_rules.description: If at any time content fails to be validated, the validator must clearly signal to the user that some of the content does not match the claim, and if possible, should indicate what part of the content did not validate.
validation_rules.source_text: If at any time content fails to be validated, the validator must clearly signal to the user that some of the content does not match the claim, and if possible, should indicate what part of the content

validation_rules.description: The validator must continue to report validation has failed, even if later portions of the content validate correctly.
validation_rules.source_text: The validator must continue to report validation has failed, even if later portions of the content validate correctly.

validation_rules.description: As the content becomes available, the validator must validate each portion of the content before it is rendered as previously described.
validation_rules.source_text: As the content becomes available, the validator must validate each portion of the content before it is rendered as previously described.

validation_rules.description: In addition, the validator must validate that the sequence of said content is the same as when the manifest was produced.
validation_rules.source_text: In addition, the validator must validate that the sequence of said content is the same as when the manifest was produced.

validation_rules.description: Unless the player has explicitly signalled the validator that a discontinuity is expected (e.g., when the consumer performs a manual seek operation via the UI), the validator must clearly signal to the user that an unexpected discontinuity has occurred whenever the sequence does not match.
validation_rules.source_text: Unless the player has explicitly signalled the validator that a discontinuity is expected (e.g., when the consumer performs a manual seek operation via the UI), the validator must clearly signal to th

validation_rules.description: For content that is intentionally not being rendered as the claim generator originally intended, such as during fast-forward, rewind, or playback at a different speed, the validator may not be able to validate the content.
validation_rules.source_text: For content that is intentionally not being rendered as the claim generator originally intended, such as during fast-forward, rewind, or playback at a different speed, the validator may not be able to

validation_rules.description: In this case, the validator must clearly signal to the user that the content cannot be validated during the corresponding operation.
validation_rules.source_text: In this case, the validator must clearly signal to the user that the content cannot be validated during the corresponding operation.

constraints: Never use in informational, security, or threat analysis sections
Same concept uses the same normative verb throughout the spec

rules.meaning: Recommended but exceptions exist with clear justification.
constraints: Do not use 'it is recommended that' -- use 'should' directly
Verify it does not mask a 'should'
Network access is always optional -- any retrieval uses 'may attempt'

rules.contains: Mechanism description, data structures, CDDL schemas
rules.must_not_contain: Validation logic, security analysis, implementation guidance

rules.contains: How to validate the mechanism, status codes, error conditions
Security analysis, threat model, risk assessment

structural_placement.principle: The feature section defines the mechanism. Validation and security sections define how to check it. Guidance does not belong in normative spec text.

cddl_conventions.naming: kebab-case for all CDDL rule names (e.g. region-map, action-items-map-v2)

extensibility.forward_compat: Include '* tstr => any' wildcard in maps for forward compatibility

field_rules: CBOR diagnostic examples must match schema field names, types, and structure
Examples demonstrate the minimal valid case unless showing optional fields

new_assertion_checklist: Define data structure in feature section with CDDL schema
Add assertion label to the assertion label registry
Add validation rules to Validation.adoc specific assertion validation list
Add all status codes to consolidated status code tables (success/informational/failure)
Add security analysis to Threats_Harms.adoc if applicable
Include CBOR diagnostic example matching the schema
Verify extensibility: '* tstr => any' wildcard present

semantic_terms.manifest: The C2PA Manifest -- a signed collection of assertions about an asset
semantic_terms.manifest_store: The container holding one or more manifests
semantic_terms.active_manifest: The current (most recent) manifest in a manifest chain
semantic_terms.claim_generator: The entity creating the manifest, not a software component
semantic_terms.validator: The entity performing validation of a manifest
semantic_terms.trust: Claim signature verification against a trust list -- distinct from integrity
semantic_terms.integrity: Hash verification of content bindings -- distinct from trust
semantic_terms.componentOf: Parent-to-child relationship ONLY. Never describe as child-to-parent.
semantic_terms.parentOf: The derived-from/predecessor relationship in Update Manifests

cross_reference_rules: Every new assertion must appear in the Specific Assertion Validation list
Every new status code must appear in consolidated status code tables
hashed-uri references must use $hashed-uri-map in CDDL
Update Manifests have exactly one parentOf ingredient and no hard bindings
Network access is ALWAYS optional -- retrieval must use 'may attempt'

status_codes.meaning: The claim signature referenced in the ingredient's claim validated.
The signing credential is listed on the validator's trust list.
The time-stamp credential is listed on the validator's trust list.
The hash of the the referenced assertion in the ingredient's manifest matches the corresponding hash in the assertion's hashed URI in the claim.
Hash of a byte range of the asset matches the hash declared in the data hash assertion.
Hash of a box-based asset matches the hash declared in the BMFF hash assertion.
A non-embedded (remote) assertion was accessible at the time of validation.
The referenced claim in the ingredient's manifest cannot be found.
More than one claim box is present in the manifest.
No hard bindings are present in the claim.
A required field is not present in the claim.
The cbor of the claim is not valid
The hash of the the referenced ingredient claim in the manifest does not match the corresponding hash in the ingredient's hashed URI in the claim.
The claim signature referenced in the ingredient's claim cannot be found in its manifest.
The claim signature referenced in the ingredient's claim failed to validate.
The manifest has more than one ingredient whose `relationship` is `parentOf`.
The manifest is an update manifest, but it contains a disallowed assertion, such as a hard binding or actions assertions.
The manifest is an update manifest, but it contains either zero or multiple `parentOf` ingredients.
The signing credential is not listed on the validator's trust list.
The signing credential is not valid for signing.
The signing credential has been revoked by the issuer.
The signing credential has expired.
The time-stamp does not correspond to the contents of the claim.
The time-stamp credential is not listed on the validator's trust list.
The signed time-stamp attribute in the signature falls outside the validity window of the signing certificate or the TSA's certificate.
The hash of the the referenced assertion in the manifest does not match the corresponding hash in the assertion's hashed URI in the claim.
An assertion listed in the ingredient's claim is missing from the ingredient's manifest.
The manifest has more than one hard binding assertion.
An assertion was found in the ingredient's manifest that was not explicitly declared in the ingredient's claim.
status_codes.url_usage: C2PA Claim Box or C2PA Assertion

status_codes.meaning: A non-embedded (remote) assertion was inaccessible at the time of validation.
An assertion was declared as redacted in the ingredient's claim but is still present in the ingredient's manifest.
An assertion was declared as redacted by its own claim.
A required field is not present in an assertion.
The JSON(-LD) of an assertion is not valid
The cbor of an assertion is not valid
An `action` assertion was redacted when the ingredient's claim was created.
The hash of a byte range of the asset does not match the hash declared in the data hash assertion.
The hash of a box-based asset does not match the hash declared in a BMFF hash assertion.
A hard binding assertion is in a cloud data assertion.
An update manifest contains a cloud data assertion referencing an actions assertion.
The value of an `alg` header, or other header that specifies an algorithm used to compute the value of another field, is unknown or unsupported.
